title: oxalis areolata
intro: Oxalis areolata, also known as the wood sorrel or creeping wood sorrel, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family and is native to the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation due to its high levels of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Oxalis areolata include its ability to act as a natural diuretic, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of kidney stones. Oxalis areolata can be prepared as an infusion, tea, or tincture, and is also used as a flavoring agent in salads and other dishes.
